questions.question: The regent who looked after affairs of government on behalf of young Akbar.
The battle fought in 1556 CE that crushed Afghan power.
The chief minister and general of Adil Shah who was defeated and killed.
The Rajput chief who led the Mughal forces in the Battle of Haldighati.
The battle fought in 1576 CE between the Mughals and Rana Pratap.
The new capital city built by Akbar, referred to as a 'dream in stone'.
The hall of prayer built by Akbar in 1575 CE for religious discussions.
The new religious order created by Akbar in 1582 CE.
The only Hindu courtier who accepted Din-i-Ilahi.
The revenue minister who introduced the land revenue system (Bandobast).
The tax imposed by earlier Muslim rulers on non-Muslim subjects.
The court historian who wrote the Akbarnama.
The most famous musician at Akbar's court.
The principle meaning 'universal peace' that Akbar believed in.
The gold coin issued by Akbar.
